company_name: The Mason Group - Strategic Search Partners
job_description: Our client is a global leader in information management, where innovation, creativity, and collaboration are the key components of their corporate culture. As a critical member of their finance team, you will have the opportunity to partner with the most highly regarded companies in the world, tackle complex issues, and contribute to projects that shape the future of digital transformation. Growth has led to recruiting for a Senior Manager, M&A Finance. This role will manage financial activities throughout the deal lifecycle, including assisting with diligence and close processes.Responsibilities of the Senior Manager, Finance M&A is as follows but not limited to:Build strong working relationships to drive key finance related M&A and post close integration initiatives. This includes partnering with cross functional teams including corporate development, corporate accounting & reporting, international controllership, corporate and indirect tax, transfer pricing, treasury, financial planning & analysis, revenue recognition, legal, and operations, among others. Contribute to finance team functions during the diligence process by partnering with Corporate Development M&A to evaluate the financial merits of potential transactions. Identify cross-functional interdependencies and gaps between finance and operational teams that require attention before or after deal close.Review definitive agreements ensuring adequacy of representations and indemnities to cover financial and reporting risks identified during diligence.Support net working capital analysis and negotiations, including determining target net working capital levels and executing post-close calculations and settlements.Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop and execute transition service agreements as required. Support purchase price accounting including the preparation and accounting for purchase consideration and delivery of the opening balance sheet. Coordinate with external valuation consultants and internal business teams to assess valuation assumptions for acquired intangibles.Prepare post close legal entity valuations and record journal entries to pushdown acquired intangibles and goodwill to legal entities, as required.Contribute to the preparation of pro forma financial statements and other assigned projects. Act as a point of contact with external auditors with respect to all M&A deliverables to support the timely completion of the annual audit and quarterly reviews of the consolidated financial statements.Assist in preparing legal entity valuations for statutory legal entity mergers or liquidations, and in support of annual statutory legal entity goodwill impairment testing.Support other finance-related projects as assigned. Requirements5+ years of relevant experience in M&A and valuation roles (buy & sell side), ideally with exposure to both public accounting and industry roles in large organizations.Accounting designation required (CPA, CA, or US CPA preferred).CBV or similar valuation designation preferred.Proven self-starter with the ability to balance multiple tasks of competing priorities in a changing and dynamic environment.Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to effectively facilitate or lead cross functional teams.Proven capability to build and manage relationships with professional services partners to achieve high-quality, timely and efficient delivery of engagements when required.Experience interacting with senior management and executive levels with the ability to assess business risk and escalate to management with appropriate alternatives.Strong analytical and decision-making abilities with a high attention to detail.Additional Information:The Mason Group is committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and accessible recruitment process. We welcome applicants from all qualified candidates. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates participating in all stages of the recruitment process, in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).This role is for an immediate vacancy, and we are actively hiring. Technology-assisted screening tools including AI may be used during the recruitment process; however, all applications are reviewed by our Consultants. If you are already registered with The Mason Group, please contact your Recruiting Consultant directly with reference to job #20549.

Company DescriptionAsahi Refining is a global leader in precious metal assaying, refining, and bullion products, with nearly 200 years of heritage and expertise. The company was established when Asahi Holdings, a Japan-based precious metals recycling company founded in 1952, acquired the gold and silver refining operations of Johnson Matthey. With a presence in six countries, Asahi Refining specializes in processing gold, silver, and platinum group metals while serving diverse industries, including mining, recycling, institutional investment, and distribution. Guided by a legacy of quality and innovation, Asahi Refining is committed to building trust through long-lasting relationships and delivering superior products and services to clients worldwide.Role DescriptionThe Senior Financial Analyst plays a key role in supporting financial planning, reporting, and decision‑making across the organization. This position is responsible for analyzing financial and operational performance, preparing management reports, and updating forecasts. The Senior Financial Analyst partners closely with cross‑functional teams to improve financial performance, support strategic initiatives, and enhance financial processes and controls.PRINCIPAL ACCOUNTABILITIESDuties include the following:· Prepare and analyze monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports for management· Perform variance analysis versus budget, forecast, and prior periods; identify key drivers and trends· Partner with business stakeholders to understand underlying causes and validate assumptions· Update periodic forecasts, incorporating actual results, business assumptions, and outlook changes· Assist in the annual budgeting and long range planning processes· Develop and maintain financial models to support planning, forecasting, and scenario analysis· Assist in building, maintaining, and improving product cost models· Analyze material, labor, overhead, and other cost components· Support cost updates, standard cost reviews, and margin analysis· Partner with Operations and Accounting to ensure cost accuracy and transparency· Conduct ad hoc financial and operational analyses in support of management requests· Support annual insurance renewal degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related discipline.· CPA certification required.· 5 – 10 years of progressive experience in accounting, finance, FP&A or related analytical role.· Strong and senior leadership· Mining, manufacturing, CPG, finance or related experience is an asset.PERSONAL SKILLS/COMPETENCIES· Strong business acumen and attention to detail· Excellent written and verbal and comfortable working independently and collaboratively· Strong analytical, problem solving, organizational and time management skills.· Ability to always maintain confidentiality.

job_title: Dealer Development Manager Montreal/directeur spécialiste en développement
job_description: Le Groupe financier LGM (« LGM ») figure sur la liste des meilleurs employeurs canadiens. De plus, LGM a été nommé à plusieurs reprises par PROFIT 500 comme étant l'une des entreprises canadiennes connaissant une croissance des plus rapides! À titre de partenaire de choix du marché de l’automobile, LGM révolutionne l’expérience de propriété des consommateurs.L’entreprise :Depuis 1998, LGM, qui fournit des produits de qualité appuyés d'une excellente formation. Le partenariat établi avec ces concessionnaires bénéficie d’un appui complet de la part des fabricants automobiles suivants : BMW/MINI, Kia, Volvo, Jaguar, Land Rover/Range Rover, Mazda, Mitsubishi Motors Polestar et Motorad. LGM élabore des solutions gagnantes pour les consommateurs, les concessionnaires, les fabricants automobiles et les souscripteurs. Son processus de réclamations est juste et rapide; une approche agréable pour les consommateurs et le département de service des concessionnaires. LGM a aussi créé un Centre de ressources numériques (CRN) d'avant-garde. En fait, le CRN agit à titre de réseau principal de distribution qui simplifie les processus de vente et de remise.Le Poste :Le directeur spécialiste en développement deconcessions devra se rendre chez les concessionnaires automobiles afin de créer de nouveaux comptes et de s’occuper des comptes existants pour la vente de produits et de services LGM.Se rendre chez les concessionnaires pour établir de nouvelles relations d’affaires.Se rendre chez les concessionnaires pour entretenir les relations d’affaires existantes.Aider les concessionnaires à augmenter le rendement de leur bureau commercial.Aider les concessionnaires à augmenter la productivité ainsi que les ventes dans leur concession.Offrir aux employés de la concession une formation et un soutien sur les produits.Offrir aux clients existants et aux clients potentiels la meilleure expérience de service possible.Créer pour les clients partenaires des plans de développement personnalisés, dont les objectifs sont clairs, et contrôler les résultats.Réaliser des études sur les comptes-clés, et ce, sur une base régulière.Ce que nous recherchons Capacité de vendre intelligemment et avec éthique.Capacité de parler intelligemment de l’industrie automobile.Autonomie, bon sens de l’organisation et excellente de gestion de son temps.Capacité de comprendre et d’absorber rapidement les notions de produits.Excellente connaissance de la gamme de produits de Microsoft Office.Scolarité :Éducation postsecondaire étant un atout.Accepter de suivre le cours offert par l’Association canadienne des professionnels de la vente (ACPV).Accepter de suivre des cours en vue de l’obtention de permis requis (le cas échéant).Expérience :Preuve de rendement antérieur et de succès dans la vente.Expérience de 3 ans et plus dans l’industrie automobile et le bureau commercial.Le fait de travailler chez LGM est aussi caractérisé par les propositions de valeur suivantes :Travail d’équipe par le biais de groupes de travail stratégiques.Nous révolutionnons l’expérience de propriété des consommateurs.Nous formons nos futurs dirigeants au moyen de l'apprentissage continu.L'engagement des employés est essentiel à notre équipe hautement performante.Les gens, les processus et la technologie sont indispensables à l’élaboration de solutions novatrices.Nous encourageons la transparence et la franchise pour inspirer des idées et développer de nouvelles perspectives.Les conversations courageuses et le partage d’expériences assorti des responsabilités individuelles fonctionnent à tout coup!Les Avantages :Selon s'il s'agit d'un employé à temps plein ou à temps partiel :Nous offrons un programme très concurrentiel de rémunération et d'avantages sociaux comprenant notamment : un régime complet d'assurance-santé, des REÉR collectifs, une prime de rendement, un parrainage pour l’éducation et quatre jours rémunérés pour faire du bénévolat dans la communauté. Nous payons également l’abonnement de l’employé à un centre de conditionnement physique. De plus, nous offrons un programme de remise à l'achat d'un véhicule.Les Gens :LGM figure, depuis les huit dernières, sur la liste des meilleurs employeurs canadiens de l'institut Great Place to WorkMD. Les employés motivés, créatifs et hautement qualifiés sont les raisons de notre succès.Nos employés rendent cela possible grâce à leur engagement inébranlable envers une culture d'innovation et de collaboration.Les services et les innovations de LGM contribuent à la confiance et à la fidélisation.Joignez-vous à l’équipe!The CompanyLGM is a national leader in providing warranty, finance, and insurance services to the Canadian automotive industry. Since 1998, LGM has been successfully supplying quality products and leading training solutions to Canadian car dealerships. Dealer partnerships are complemented with the strong backing and support of their automotive manufacturing brands, which include BMW/MINI, Kia, Mazda, Volvo, Jaguar/Land Rover, Mitsubi

job_description: At AstraZeneca, we pride ourselves on crafting a collaborative culture that champions knowledge-sharing, ambitious thinking and innovation – ultimately providing employees with the opportunity to work across teams, functions and even the globe.Our dedication to sustainability is also central to our culture and part of what makes AstraZeneca a great place to work. We know the health of people, the planet and our business are interconnected which is why we’re taking ambitious action to tackle some of the biggest challenges of our time, from climate change to access to healthcare and disease prevention.Introduction to roleThe Clinical Research Associate (CRA) has local responsibility for the delivery of studies at allocated sites and is an active participant in the local study team(s). The CRA works closely with other CRAs and the Local Study Team/Local Study Associate Director (LSAD) to ensure that study commitments are achieved in a timely and efficient manner. Acting as the main contact with the study site, the CRA is responsible for monitoring the study conduct to ensure proper delivery. The CRA prepares, initiates, monitors, and closes assigned sites in clinical studies, following AZ Procedural Documents, international guidelines such as ICH-GCP, and relevant local regulations. A CRA with longer tenure and experience may take on additional responsibilities associated with LSAD.AccountabilitiesContributes to the selection of potential investigators.In some countries, as required, CRAs are accountable for study start-up and regulatory maintenance. Tasks may include Site Qualification Visits, collection, preparation, review and tracking of documents for the application process; submission of proper application/documents to EC/IRB and to Regulatory Authorities for start-up and for the duration of the study.Trains, supports and advises Investigators and site staff in study-related matters, including Risk Based Quality Management (RbQM) principles.Confirms that site staff have completed and documented the required trainings appropriately, including ICH-GCP training, prior to and for the duration of the study. Ensures the sites are inspection ready at all times.Actively participates in Local Study Team (LST) meetings.Contributes to National Investigators meetings, as applicable.Initiates, monitors and closes study sites in compliance with AZ Procedural Documents. Shares information on patient recruitment and study site progress (site quality/performance) within the LST.Drives performance at the sites. Proactively identifies and ensures timely resolution to study-related issues and escalates them as appropriate.Updates CTMS and other systems with data from study sites as per required timelines.Manages study supplies (ISF, etc), drug supplies and drug accountability at study site. Prepares study drug for destruction, if applicable.Performs monitoring visits (remote and onsite), as well as remote data checks, in accordance with the timelines specified in the study-specific Monitoring Plan. If required, determines and discusses with LSAD the correct timing and type of visits.Performs Source Data Review (SDR), Case Report Form (CRF) review and Source Data Verification (SDV), in accordance with the Monitoring Plan.Performs regular Site Quality Risk Assessments and adapts monitoring intensity accordingly during the study.Ensures data query resolution in a timely manner.Works with data management to ensure robust quality of the collected study data.Ensures accurate and timely reporting of Serious Adverse Events and their follow-ups.Prepares and finalises monitoring visit reports in CTMS and provides timely feedback to the Principal Investigator, including follow-up letter, within required timelines and in line with AZ SOP.Follows up on outstanding actions with study sites to ensure resolution in a timely manner.Follows quality issue processes by escalating systematic or serious quality issues, data privacy breaches, CSP or ICH-GCP compliance issues to Local Management and/or CQM as required.Assists site in maintaining inspection-ready ISF.Prepares for and collaborates with activities associated with audits and regulatory inspections in liaison with LSAD and CQAD.Ensures timely collection/uploading of essential documents into the eTMF in accordance with ICH-GCP, AZ SOPs and local requirements. Supports/participates in regular QC checks performed by LSAD or delegate.Ensures that all study documents under their responsibility (i.e., site documents, relevant communications, etc.) are available and ready for final archiving and completion of local part of the eTMF.Provides feedback on any research-related information including sites/investigators/competing studies that might be useful for the local market.Ensures compliance with AstraZeneca’s Code of Ethics, company policies and procedures relating to people, finance, technology, security and SHE (Safety, Health and Environment).Ensures compliance with local, national and re

Who we are: kreitmaker is a rapidly growing masonry and concrete supplier in Toronto, Ontario. We pride ourselves on providing excellent customer service and offering unique and innovative products to the Canadian construction landscape. Who we are looking for: We are seeking a Finance Manager who is eager to lead and grow with our business. The ideal candidate is a detail-oriented individual with expertise in accounting, cash flow management, and financial forecasting. The chosen candidate will enjoy work-life balance and work in an environment with a positive and supportive culture. Role summary: The Finance Manager is responsible for assisting the Financial Controller with managing the overall financial operations for kreitmaker and its related entities. This role will oversee accounting operations, financial reporting, and analysis. This role involves ensuring financial accuracy, optimizing processes, managing inventory, and providing strategic recommendations to drive our company's financial success. With a focus on leadership and innovation, this position offers an exciting opportunity to help shape our financial future and contribute to our ongoing growth and success in the construction industry. Location and hours: This is an onsite role, working mainly out of our 31 Industrial Street location, Monday to Friday 8:00 am – 4:00 pm. This role will also be required to work out of our other GTA locations when needed. Roles and Responsibilities Accounting Operations & Financial Reporting Oversee accounting operations, including reviewing journal entries, monthly remittances (e.g., HST, WSIB), payroll, accounts payable, and accounts receivable. Administer funds according to approved budgets and monitor financial expenditures. Assist the Controller in preparing period-end financial statements accurately and in a timely manner. Develop and maintain accounting policies and procedures. Optimize the use of accounting systems and develop and distribute training in accounting software and other accounting policies and processes to impacted business users. Financial Planning & Analysis Develop and manage budgets, forecasts, and financial models. job_title: Agent de bureau - nouvelles affaires et fonds externes
job_description: Titre interne officiel:Agent de bureauStatut:Temporaire (Durée déterminée)Sommaire:Travail en présentiel au bureau de Québec à temps pleinEn tant qu'agent(e) de bureau, vous traiterez le courrier reçu/à expédier et numériserez tous les documents destinés au traitement par les diverses équipes de la direction principale Services financiers. Vous effectuerez du travail administratif relié aux documents à numériser et au workflow. Vous effectuerez certaines tâches non financières au sein de la direction dans le processus du traitement des transactions.Vous vous réaliserez dans les fonctions suivantes : Recevoir le courrier externe et inter-service : trier par direction, clientèle et/ou type de documents. job_title: Scientifique de données - Risque de crédit
job_description: À propos de nousLeader en financement automobile, Iceberg Finance est une Fintech ayant connu une croissance exceptionnelle au cours des dernières années, notamment grâce à l'acquisition majeure de la firme iFinance Canada en 2022. Finaliste en 2022 et 2023 parmi les meilleurs prêteurs selon la Canadian Lender Association (CLA), Iceberg Finance ambitionne de figurer parmi les trois principaux prêteurs spécialisés et alternatifs au Canada dans la prochaine année. Notre mission : offrir des solutions de financement simples, rapides et flexibles.Description du posteEn tant que Scientifique des données – risque de crédit, vous jouerez un rôle clé dans la transformation des données en informations exploitables qui permettront d’améliorer les décisions de crédit, le suivi du portefeuille de prêts et la satisfaction des clients. Votre mission : extraire, analyser, interpréter et valoriser les données afin de soutenir les décisions d’affaires et de gestion des risques à tous les niveaux de l’organisation.Vous collaborerez étroitement avec plusieurs équipes internes pour bien comprendre leurs besoins, traduire les données en pistes concrètes, et proposer des recommandations éclairées. Vous contribuerez également à l’amélioration continue des processus analytiques.Vos responsabilitésCollaborer avec les équipes d’affaires pour identifier des opportunités d’analyse ou d’optimisation par les données.Extraire, nettoyer et structurer les données issues de différentes sources internes et externes.Analyser les données relatives aux clients, aux transactions et aux comportements afin d'évaluer la solvabilité et le risque de défaut.Réaliser des analyses descriptives et diagnostiques pour détecter des tendances, anomalies ou leviers de performance, notamment en matière de risque de crédit.Concevoir des modèles analytiques (prédictifs ou explicatifs) pour orienter les décisions stratégiques.Proposer et surveiller les indicateurs de performance de portefeuille de prêts.Créer et maintenir des tableaux de bord interactifs (KPI) à l’aide d’outils de visualisation comme Streamlit/Shiny/Power BI.Présenter clairement les analyses et recommandations, en français et en anglais, à divers publics internes.Proposer des améliorations aux méthodes et processus d’analyse, pour gagner en efficacité et en pertinence.Profil recherchéMaitrise en science des données, ingénierie financière, statistique, économétrie, économie quantitative, finance quantitative ou discipline connexe (Un baccalauréat avec de l’expérience pourrait également être considéré).Expérience pertinente en science des données, avec compréhension des enjeux d’affaires.Connaissances des stratégies de gestion, de l’analytique, et du suivi du risque de créditTitre FRM, PRM, CFA (obtenu ou en cours) ou toute autre certification pertinente (atout).Connaissances avancées en R / Python / SQLCompréhension des notions de finance (atout)Connaissance en apprentissage machine (Machine Learning)Excellente capacité à vulgariser des concepts complexes, à l’écrit comme à l’oralExpérience avec Streamlit/Shiny/Power BI ou autre outil de visualisationConnaissances de IFRS 9/ECL/Stress testing (atout)Bilinguisme français / anglais requisCe qui vous attend chez Iceberg FinanceDes projets stimulants dans un environnement agile et en transformation.Une équipe collaborative, bienveillante et passionnée.Des bureaux modernes, avec un gym sur place.Une réelle opportunité d’influencer les décisions d’affaires par vos analyses.Nos avantagesREER collectif avec contribution de l’employeur.Assurances collectives complètes dès l’embauche (santé, dentaire, invalidité, vie).3 semaines de vacances cumulable dans l’année, Congés mobilesÉvénements d’équipe et culture d’entreprise dynamique.Stationnement gratuit sur place.Envie de faire parler les données, d’influencer les décisions de crédit et de participer activement à notre croissance ?Postulez dès maintenant et faites partie de l’aventure Iceberg Finance.

RequiredAs part of its activities, Desjardins Group uses a large number of quantitative models to support its product and service offer and to properly manage its risks, with the goal of always doing what’s best for members and clients. While these models provide indisputable decision-making support, their use leads to further risks that must in turn be analyzed, assessed and mitigated. To this end, a model-specific governance framework must be in place throughout their life cycle—from design to removal—including performance monitoring and various other development phases. As a senior advisor, model risk monitoring, you contribute to the analysis, research and development of model risk management frameworks. You provide advice, support and leadership for users, designers and model validators so that they can provide an overview of the risks associated with Desjardins’s range of models. You propose solutions to improve or optimize model governance management frameworks and tools to strengthen the controls applied to reduce model risk. You prepare recommendations, solutions and action plans to enhance model risk reporting. You help develop positions on complex situations involving models that require analysis and sufficient knowledge of the model’s area of application. Since coordination is critical, you interact with key players working in other fields related to models. You serve as a specialist advisor and subject matter expert in model governance. You also serve as a resource person and assistant for decision-making bodies. More specifically, you will be required to:Help set up and maintain the model governance framework covering the entire model life cycleContribute to the sound management of model risk throughout the model life cycle, in collaboration with the various teams involvedUpdate the annual model risk assessment and suggest improvements to the methodology as neededProduce the specific analyses required for this riskMaintain an inventory of models and obtain the required information from stakeholdersParticipate in annual planning to support model governance and propose an annual plan to update the inventoryDocument the process for updating the inventory and model riskProvide business intelligence by developing dashboards that include risk appetite indicatorsContribute to the cross-sector process that helps operationalize model governance and resolve issues in terms of model-routing, where applicableAct as superuser for the model risk governance toolFollow up on the findings issued with respect to the models to assess their business impactRegularly monitor model risk governance practices, drawing on your contacts in the industry and related publicationsWhat we offer*Competitive salary and annual bonus4 weeks of flexible vacation starting in the first yearDefined benefit pension plan that provides predictable, stable income throughout retirementGroup insurance including telemedicineReimbursement of health and wellness expenses and telework equipment Benefits apply based on eligibility criteria.What You Bring To The TableBachelor's degree in a related fieldA minimum of six years of relevant experiencePlease note that other combinations of qualifications and relevant experience may be consideredKnowledge of French is requiredIntermediate knowledge of oral and written English due to the nature of the duties or work tools or because the position involves interactions with English-speaking partners, members and/or clientsExpertise in financial institution risk managementProject management expertiseKnowledge and understanding of regulations related to model risk managementKnowledge of quantitative modellingKnowledge of business intelligence tools Communicates effectively, Drive results, Interpersonal Savvy, Strategic mindsetTrade Union (If applicable)At Desjardins, we believe in equity, diversity and inclusion. We're committed to welcoming, respecting and valuing people for who they are as individuals, learning from their differences, embracing their uniqueness, and providing a positive workplace for all. At Desjardins, we have zero tolerance for discrimination of any kind. We believe our teams should reflect the diversity of the members, clients and communities we serve.If there's something we can do to help make the recruitment process or the job you're applying for more accessible, let us know. We can provide accommodations at any stage in the recruitment process. Just ask!Job FamilyRisk management (FG)Unposting Date2026-04-3

job_title: Actuarial Analyst or Senior Actuarial Analyst
job_description: DescriptionJob TitleActuarial Analyst or Senior Actuarial Analyst18 months contractBuild the future with usAre you driven by the insurance and financial security sector and eager to contribute to the purpose of a company that aims to help its clients feel confident and secure about their future? As an Actuarial Analyst or Senior Actuarial Analyst, you will play a key role in analyzing the profitability of group savings and pension products, while contributing to the business line’s profitable growth strategy.This position is a great opportunity to showcase your skills and fully realize your potential within a caring and trustworthy company. Here, people and their development are at the heart of our priorities, fostering an environment that encourages collaboration and innovation.What You’ll Accomplish With UsAs an Actuarial Analyst or Senior Actuarial Analyst, you’ll be at the core of our mission. Here are the main responsibilities:Participate in the development, enhancement, and optimization of actuarial models to support business decision-making.Perform experience studies and contribute to the development of actuarial assumptions for group savings and pension products.Collaborate in the development of pricing strategies for the Group Insurance, Savings and Retirement Solutions (SAEC) sector, with a focus on profitable growth.Contribute to the design and calculation of various compensation programs related to products and business lines.Improve analytical processes and tools to generate gains in efficiency, quality, and reliability of results.Participate in various strategic initiatives, including the preparation of the business line budget and the formulation of recommendations aimed at optimizing profitability while ensuring sound risk management.What Could Accelerate Your Success In This RoleWe’re looking for someone who:Is known for their strong understanding of the Canadian pension system.Stands out for their experience in using and developing complex financial projection models.Is recognized for proficiency in Microsoft Office, leveraged to structure, analyze, and communicate information effectively.Holds a bachelor’s degree in actuarial science.Completion of one or more actuarial exams (SOA or CIA) is considered an asset.Proficiency in both French and English is required, as the role involves weekly written communication with French-speaking teams and English-only teams.Why you’ll love working with usA work environment where learning and development merge with a collective pursuit of excellence.A healthy, safe, fair, and inclusive environment where potential can be freely expressed and developed.The opportunity to work in a hybrid environment, supported by flexibility and access to inspiring and innovative workspaces.Competitive benefits: Flexible group insurance, competitive pension plan, stock purchase plan, vacation and wellness/personal development days, telemedicine, employee and family assistance program, ergonomic furniture program, performance bonus, discounts on iA products, and much more!Apply now and get ahead of your career, where your talent really belongs! Still unsure about applying?At iA, we believe in potential and value diverse experiences. If this role inspires you, go ahead and apply – your place might be with us, and we want to get to know you!

QC, Quebec City - LebourgneufThe world of work is changing…short commutes, flexibility, and convenience are the minimum standards employees want when it comes to how they want to work!At IWG, we are leading the way. We’ve built the world’s largest workspace network so that our customers can work from wherever, and however, they want… in over 120 countries across the globe!Join us and help us bring more freedom to people and businesses, while expanding our network. You’ll be able to enjoy a great work life balance and competitive benefits, while growing your career path along the way.The opportunityAs a Community Associate, you’ll work closely with new and existing customers across a myriad of industries, working to create and maintain your own community within your centre, where you will help take care of all the administrative and support needs for your customers…so that your customers can focus on running their business.A typical day at IWG You arrive a few minutes before your centre opens to make sure everything is ready and check there’s nothing the cleaners have missed.Customers arrive all throughout the morning. One asks you for a changed Wi-Fi code. Another wants to know if his important package has arrived. A woman needs directions to her meeting room… and can you help her set up the projector and show her where to find a great cup of coffee. You’re off to help her get set-up, as soon as you connect the incoming call to another customer. You may sure that all of the services are properly charged, so that invoices are accurate.It’s time for a quick coffee break, relax and enjoy a few minutes to yourself.The mail arrives. You sort through it while dealing with a whole host of other customer requests, remembering to email the customer from earlier to let him know his package is waiting for him in his mailbox. And that’s lunch.A new wave of customers arrive throughout the afternoon. You help a customer with printing and another with their invoice. A customer in a team room say it’s too hot in their office, no problem you are on it and open a ticket in our service portal.You’ve earned yourself another quick coffee break, relax and enjoy a few minutes to yourself.Early afternoon, you sit down with the Community Manager to discuss the organization of next week’s networking event in your centre. You plan and write the e-invites to customers, then create, print and put-up posters on the noticeboards around the centre. To your excitement, the Community Manager is coming around the corner with a prospect (someone interested in joining the IWG community), you share a few details about what makes this space so amazing for your customers and encourage them to join the network soon so they can participate in next week’s networking event - then you head back to finish putting up your posters.The day is coming to a close. Time to gently ask the large group in meeting room 3 to start wrapping up. You direct them to the nice restaurant you know around the corner, so they can grab a bite and continue their discussion. You ensure everything’s nice and tidy, it’s time to head home.About YouWe’re looking for someone who knows how to manage multiple tasks while providing customers with the best possible service. job_description: Cap carrière – Personnel de soutien | CSSDNCap carrière – Personnel de soutienAffichage interne et externe📰 Numéro de concours: S-2526-214 💼 Type de poste: Banque de candidatures 📍 Lieu: Centre administratif du CSSDN 🔢 Horaire: 16h à 19h 💰 Taux horaire: Selon la convention collective en vigueur 🗓️ Date d’entrée en fonction: Jeudi 2 avril 2026 ⌛ Échéance de l’affichage: Mercredi 01 avril 2026 DescriptionParticipez à notre événement de recrutement pour rencontrer notre équipe d’acquisition de talents, découvrir les opportunités de carrière au sein de nos établissements et échanger avec notre personnel des ressources humaines, qui sera présent pour répondre à toutes vos questions.Profils Recherchés Soutien technique et para techniqueTechnicienne ou technicien en éducation spécialiséePréposée ou préposé aux élèves handicapésSurveillante ou surveillant d’élèvesSoutien administratifSecrétaireAgente ou agent de bureauQualifications RequisesScolaritéÊtre titulaire d’un diplôme d’études en lien avec l’emploi sollicité ou être titulaire d’un diplôme ou d’une attestation d’études dont l’équivalence est reconnue par l’autorité compétente.AvantagesAlternance travail études possibleDes assurances collectivesUn régime de retraite à prestations déterminéesLa période des fêtes en congéUn programme d’aide aux employésStationnement gratuitInscriptionAvant de soumettre votre candidature, veuillez vous assurer de remplir les critères de formation requis pour le poste. Préparez un curriculum vitæ à jour et, si possible, une lettre de motivation. N’oubliez pas d’inclure vos diplômes ou relevés de notes pertinents. ⌛Les personnes souhaitant participer au concours doivent soumettre leur candidature au plus tard le mercredi 01 avril 2026.Toutes les candidatures sont traitées confidentiellement. Puisque nous valorisons le développement de notre personnel, les candidatures internes sont considérées en premier. Nous nous réservons le droit de mettre fin à l’affichage de l’offre d’emploi lorsque le poste est pourvu. Nous communiquons uniquement avec les personnes retenues pour une entrevue.En plus d’encourager l’équité, la diversité, l’inclusion et l’accessibilité dans nos environnements de travail, nous appliquons un programme d’accès à l’égalité à l’emploi. Nous invitons donc les femmes, les membres de toutes les minorités (visibles, ethniques, etc.), les Autochtones et les personnes avec handicap (visible ou invisible) à présenter leur candidature. Les personnes handicapées qui le désirent peuvent recevoir de l’assistance pour le processus de présélection et de sélection. Nous sommes également soumis à l’application de la Loi sur la laïcité de l’État. Dans le cadre du processus de sélection et d’évaluation, les personnes ayant posé leur candidature pourront être soumises à des tests.Le CSSDN : inclusif, innovant et inspirantChez les Navigateurs, nous croyons en l’éducation de nos élèves, mais aussi de nos membres du personnel. Notre vision, offrir des parcours de réussite pour tout le monde, dans des milieux inclusifs, innovants et inspirants est au coeur de notre organisation. Nos 78 établissements (écoles primaires et secondaires, centres de formation professionnelle et d’éducation des adultes, ainsi que nos services administratifs) rassemblent plus de 30 000 élèves et près de 5 000 membres du personnel. Ils sont situés dans la magnifique région de la Chaudière-Appalaches sur un territoire qui s’étend sur environ 90 km, le long de la rive sud du fleuve Saint-Laurent.Voyez grand pour votre carrière!capsurvospossibilites.caCoordonnées des servicesadministratifs du CSSDN1860, 1re RueLévis (Québec) G6W 5M6Téléphone : 418 839-0500Réseaux sociauxfacebookgoogleplusgoogleplusgoogleplusParentsAdmission et inscriptionMozaïk-Portail-ParentsTrouver une écoleTransport scolaireCap sur le secondaireMembres du personnelMicrosoft 365IntranetServices éducatifsMozaïk-PortailLiens rapidesCalendriers scolairesCarrièreFaire une plainteFormation professionnelle des NavigateursMoodlePolitique de confidentialitéSalle de presseTaxe scolaireCode d’éthiqueCarrièreCalendriers scolairesFormation professionnelle des NavigateursIntranetNous joindreTaxe scolaireTransport scolaireMinistère de l’Éducation© Gouvernement du Québec, 2026

About The JobThe Varsity Tutors Live Learning Platform has thousands of students looking for online CFA - Level 1 tutors nationally. As a tutor on the Varsity Tutors Platform, you'll have the flexibility to set your own schedule, earn competitive rates, and make a real impact on students' academic success and understanding. All from the comfort of your home.Why Join Our Platform?Earn incrementally higher pay for each session with the same student, reaching up to $40/hour.Get paid up to twice per week, ensuring fast and reliable compensation for the tutoring sessions you conduct and invoice.Set your own hours and tutor as much as you'd like.Tutor remotely using our purpose-built Live Learning Platform. No commuting required.Get matched with students best-suited to your teaching style and expertise.Our AI-powered Tutor Copilot enhances your sessions with real-time instructional support, lesson generation, and engagement features, helping you save prep time and focus on impactful teaching.We handle the logistics—you just invoice for your tutoring sessions, and we take care of payments.What We Look For In a CFA - Level 1 TutorAdvanced Test Mastery: Deep knowledge of CFA Level 1 examination content covering ethical and professional standards, quantitative methods, economics, financial statement analysis, corporate issuers, equity investments, fixed income, derivatives, alternative investments, and portfolio management. Ability to explain time value of money, financial ratio analysis, and the CFA Institute Code of Ethics while preparing candidates for chartered financial analyst certification.Strategic Test-Taking & Problem-Solving: Skilled at teaching financial statement analysis, quantitative problem solving, and ethics application for CFA Level 1 examination. Guides students through computing financial ratios, applying quantitative methods, analyzing fixed income and equity valuation, understanding derivative pricing, and applying the Standards of Professional Conduct to scenarios. Emphasizes the CFA exam weighting and targeting high-weight topics for score optimization.Curriculum Awareness & Adaptive Instruction: Familiar with CFA Level 1 content and common challenges such as the breadth of ten topic areas, ethics scenario complexity, and quantitative calculation requirements. Adapts instruction using CFA Institute study materials, practice examinations, and topic-focused review to support finance professionals preparing for CFA Level 1 with structured study plans targeting the minimum passing score.Effective Teaching Methods: Ability to identify concepts students commonly struggle with, explain material using multiple approaches, and adapt instruction to meet individual learning needs and styles.Strong communication skills and a friendly, engaging teaching style.Ability to adapt to different learning styles and student needs.Ways To Connect With Students1-on-1 Online Tutoring - Provide personalized instruction to individual students.Instant Tutoring - Accept on-demand tutoring requests whenever you're available.About Varsity Tutors And 1-on-1 Online TutoringOur mission is to transform the way people learn by leveraging advanced technology, AI, and the latest in learning science to create personalized learning experiences. Through 1-on-1 Online Tutoring, students receive customized instruction that helps them achieve their learning goals. Our platform is designed to match students with the right tutors, fostering better outcomes and a passion for learning.Please note: Varsity Tutors does not contract in: Alaska, California, Colorado, Delaware, Hawaii, Maine, New Hampshire, North Dakota, Vermont, West Virginia or Puerto Rico., #CFA-Level1, #Education, #Remote, #OnlineTutoring

job_description: Bentley SystemsLocation: Quebec (Office-based, Quebec )Hours: Monday–Friday, 08:30–17:00 (37.5 hours/week)Position SummaryAt Bentley, we believe that an exceptional workplace is the foundation of our success. We are looking for a passionate and proactive Workplace Culture & Engagement Specialist to be the central point of contact and the cultural heart of our Quebec office.This is a newly created role designed for someone who is excited to take ownership of our workplace environment and being a key person to help shape our culture. You will be empowered to create a vibrant, inclusive, and seamless office experience that reflects Bentley's values and makes our Quebec hub a fantastic place to work.You will be the first point of contact for all visitors, including global colleagues and executives, while overseeing the day‑to‑day running of our Quebec office. You will champion culture initiatives, support global programs like Sustainability, IDEA, Bentley Giving, and Achievers, and help design engaging in-office experiences.ResponsibilitiesFront of House & Workplace Excellence:Act as the primary point of contact for the Quebec office, embodying Bentley's brand, and values as the first impression for all colleagues and external visitors. Oversee daily office operations, from managing correspondence and supplies to coordinating meeting rooms and hospitality, ensuring our facilities are always welcoming and professional. Take ownership of the workplace environment, proactively identifying opportunities to improve our space and processes to create a seamless and positive experience for everyone. Provide support services to all regularly visiting Bentley Executives, including meal reservations, transportation coordination, and other support as required. Create and process purchase requests and invoices. Culture & Community ChampionServe as a cultural ambassador, bringing our company values to life through creative and engaging in-office events and activitiesChampion and localize our global culture programs, including initiatives for Sustainability, IDEA (Inclusion, Diversity, Equity & Allyship), Bentley Giving, and our Achievers recognition platformFoster a strong sense of community and belonging, ensuring every colleague feels connected, seen, and celebrated Onboarding & Swag ManagementOrganize welcome packs and oversee first-day coordination for new employees, while also administering inventory and distribution of branded merchandise to staff and coordinating for events QualificationsMinimum 3 years’ experience in a similar role such as founders assistant, concierge, reception, facilities, or office management Strong organizational and multitasking capabilities A high degree of professionalism, discretion, and an engaging presence, with a passion for exceptional service Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage at all levels of the business up to C-suite Proficiency in Microsoft Office 365 Suite High attention to detail and a proactive, solution-oriented mindset Positive, adaptable, and collaborative team player with a “can‑do” approach What We OfferA great Team and culture – please see our colleague video. An exciting career as an integral part of a world-leading software company providing solutions for architecture, engineering, and construction - watch this short documentary about how we got our start. An attractive salary and benefits package. A commitment to inclusion, belonging and colleague wellbeing through global initiatives and resource groups. A company committed to making a real difference by advancing the world’s infrastructure for better quality of life, where your contributions help build a more sustainable, connected, and resilient world. Discover our latest user success stories for an insight into our global impact. About Bentley SystemsAround the world, infrastructure professionals rely on software from Bentley Systems to help them design, build, and operate better and more resilient infrastructure for transportation, water, energy, cities, and more. Founded in 1984 by engineers for engineers, Bentley is the partner of choice for engineering firms and owner-operators worldwide, with software that spans engineering disciplines, industry sectors, and all phases of the infrastructure lifecycle. Through our digital twin solutions, we help infrastructure professionals unlock the value of their data to transform project delivery and asset performance. www.bentley.comEqual Opportunity EmployerBentley is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and considers for employment all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, gender/gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, marital status, religion/belief, national origin, caste, age, or any other characteristic protected by local law or unrelated to job qualifications.

Titre interne officiel:Adjoint administratifStatut:RégulierSommaire:Relevant du vice-président – Finances corporatives, vous aurez à l’assister, ainsi que son équipe, en assurant un soutien technique proactif dans l’exécution des tâches administratives en mettant à profit votre sens de la collaboration, de la débrouillardise et de l’initiative. Vous apporterez votre soutien dans la priorisation et le contrôle des activités quotidiennes telles que la gestion de la correspondance, de l’agenda, de l’organisation d’événements et de la production de documents. De plus, vous réaliserez des mandats plus spécifiques aux finances, et effectuerez certains travaux à caractère confidentiel.Vous vous réaliserez dans les fonctions suivantesAssurer l’organisation ainsi qu’un suivi rigoureux de l’agenda du Vice-président. Tenir à jour l’agenda électronique, convocations de réunions statutaires et autres, supporter l’équipe de direction dans la planification et la coordination de certaines rencontres, tant à l’interne qu’à l’externe. Planifier, organiser et prendre en charge la logistique des rencontres sous la responsabilité du vice-président, et rédiger les comptes rendus du comité de gestion de la vice-présidence et autres réunions au besoin. Manipuler des documents et des dossiers confidentiels. Planifier différentes réunions financières trimestrielles et élaborer le calendrier de suivi de celles-ci en lien avec les diverses instances. Effectuer le suivi d’approbation des rapports financiers annuels audités. Procéder à la distribution de plusieurs rapports financiers, mensuels, trimestriels et annuels, et planifier le dépôt de certains sur le site Web. Produire divers documents financiers et présentations pour le Conseil d’administration et ses comités, et coordonner le dépôt de la documentation requise, dans les délais prescrits, pour chacun d’eux. Préparer les lettres, les notes de service, les rapports, les présentations et autres documents, et assurer la bonne qualité du français et de l’anglais. Collaborer étroitement avec la direction de la fiscalité dans le traitement d’un dossier spécifique qui demandera un suivi rigoureux, une confidentialité stricte de l’information traitée et la préparation de certains formulaires. Participer activement à l’élaboration des présentations PowerPoint et à la production de certains formulaires en PDF dynamique. Planifier les voyages d’affaires (réservations de chambres d’hôtel, d’avion et de train) et compléter les notes de frais du vice-président. Préparer les bons de commande et les paiements de factures à des fournisseurs. Réserver des salles de réunion et voir à l’organisation de celles-ci, dont les salles requises pour les besoins d’audit annuel pour les auditeurs externes. Participer à la planification et à la préparation du dossier d’audit annuel et à la production de divers documents requis par les auditeurs externes dans le cadre de leur audit. Coordonner et produire le dossier de déclaration de dépôts garantis pour l’AMF. Gérer le dépôt et les mises à jour des documents financiers sur le site intranet de la Vice-présidence. Vos talents et qualifications :Diplôme d’études collégiales en techniques bureautique ou en secrétariat. Minimum de 7 ans d’expérience pertinente comme adjoint(e) de direction au sein de grande organisationMaîtrise avancée des outils de la suite MS Office ( Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Adobe Acrobat)Démontrer de la facilité dans la rédaction de textes et la préparation de présentations PPTMaîtrise avancée de la langue française tant à l’oral qu’à l’écrit et une connaissance avancée de l'anglais, en raison de la nature des tâches, des outils de travail ou d’interactions avec des collègues, partenaires, clients ou fournisseurs anglophones, ou afin de répondre aux objectifs de croissance de l’entreprise#S1Beneva souscrit au programme d’accès à l’égalité en emploi et encourage les membres des groupes visés à savoir les femmes, les personnes handicapées, les autochtones et les minorités visibles, à soumettre leur candidature. Raison d'être : Les gens sont au cœur de nos actions. Nous les accompagnons dans leurs projets de vie en leur apportant la tranquillité d'esprit et nous contribuons au bien-être de la collectivité. Suivez-nous sur Instagram ! @beneva.ca

Brand DescriptionBiogénie Canada, une filiale du groupe Ortec est le chef de file mondiale de la décontamination des sites et des sols qui propose une offre complète dans le secteur de l’économie circulaire. Par son approche de traitement, de gestion et de valorisation de ressources réutilisable, Biogénie est le leader au Canada dans le traitement et la valorisation des matières résiduelles organiques, la valorisation de matières fertilisantes et agricoles, le traitement et la valorisation des sols contaminés, la restaurations et valorisation de friches industrielles.L’entreprise compte plusieurs points de service et d’installations, qu’il s’agisse de bureaux ou de centres de traitement, de recyclage et de compostage. Grâce à nos bureaux répartis à travers l’est du Canada, nous comptons sur un excellent positionnement stratégique pour soutenir des projets de petite et de grande envergure, à différentes étapes dans de le secteur de l’environnement.Job DescriptionNous recherchons pour notre département de valorisation agricole un/e Adjoint(e) administratif(ve) et technique. Rattaché(e) à la directrice adjointe de l’équipe valorisation, nous recherchons une personne polyvalente, dotée d’un esprit analytique rapide et d’une excellente capacité d’organisation. Collaborer avec la Directrice adjointe et son équipe pour comprendre leurs besoins et fournir de l’aide administrative et technique;Ce que nous pouvons t’offrir :Salaire attractif selon profil et expériencePoste permanent, 37.5h par semaine, quart de jourAssurances collectives dès l’embaucheREER avec contribution de l’employeurAllocation annuelle pour encourager la pratique d’activités sportivesTélé médecineProgramme d’aide aux employésTélétravail (1 journée par semaine)Profile DescriptionDEC en administration, en environnement ou toute autre spécialité connexe si l’expérience est pertinente;Minimum de 3 ans d’expérience en soutien administratifExpérience dans un environnement opérationnel, technique, logistique ou industriel (atout majeur);Maitrise de la suite Office (Excel, Outlook etc…) et aisance à travailler avec des systèmes informatiques internes (bases de données et logiciels propres à l’entreprise);Maîtrise du français (écrit et parlé), Anglais (un atout)Permis de conduire valide (déplacements ponctuels à prévoir)Débrouillardise et curiosité pour comprendre les opérations, afin de saisir les enjeux réels et proposer des solutions adaptées. Capacité à anticiper les besoins des opérations et à proposer des solutions pertinentesAptitude démontrée à comprendre rapidement les processus;Autonomie, rigueur et sens de l’organisation;

Nous recrutons une Adjointe administrative pour notre client à Québec (sur site 1-2 jours/semaine).Le poste offre 4 semaines de vacances, des assurances et REER, et un bon esprit d'équipe.Exigences :• Posséder 2+ ans d’expérience dans un poste similaire.• Avoir une bonne connaissance de la suite Microsoft Office.Responsabilités :• Fournir un soutien administratif essentiel pour répondre aux besoins de l’organisation et des projets en cours.• Préparer, envoyer et effectuer le suivi des factures liées aux activités professionnelles.• Collaborer avec l’équipe marketing en offrant un soutien administratif pour les initiatives et les médias sociaux.• Participer activement à la gestion des projets spéciaux en fournissant un support administratif.• Transcrire, corriger et mettre en page les rapports professionnels avant leur envoi aux clients externes.• Réviser les documents pour garantir une qualité linguistique et grammaticale irréprochable.• Assister à des réunions, rédiger des comptes rendus et préparer les procès-verbaux.• Effectuer diverses tâches connexes pour soutenir efficacement les gestionnaires et les opérations administratives.

Prepare and entry of month end transactions into general ledger via journal vouchers.2. Perform balance sheet analysis and assist Financial Controller in other month end closing processes.3. Prepare and remit GST, HST, QST, and WSIB on a monthly basis.5. Reconcile all bank accounts and all other balance sheet accounts (Sundry Receivable, Prepaid, Accrued Liabilities, etc.) on a monthly basis.6. Prepare and review impact of foreign exchange on the translation of cash and intercompany accounts with Senior Financial Analyst (SFA).7. Perform inter-company reconciliations and assist with coding of the invoices.8. Perform upload of monthly financials into Onestream, LV7 (Consolidation Software) & SharePoint.9. Preparation of periodical tax & insurance packages for submission to internal & external stakeholders.10. Assist in extracting & providing data for quarterly & annual internal & external audits.11. Investigate departmental financials at the request of users.12. Assist Finance Controller/SFA with other ad-hoc accounting and data analytical requests.13. Prepare & ensure appropriate process documentation.REQUIREMENTS:Minimum 3 years experience in similar role within general accounting.Holding a Bachelor's degree in Finance or Accounting - must.CPA or currently in progress - preferred.Previous experience in Manufacturing industry - preferred.Technical Accounting Skills – strong knowledge of GAAP/IFRS and full-cycle accounting. - MustFinancial Analysis – ability to analyze balance sheet accounts and variances. - MustReconciliation Skills – accurate and timely bank and account reconciliations. - MustTax Knowledge – understanding of GST/HST/QST and other regulatory filings. - PreferredERP & Financial Systems – experience with accounting/ERP software (e.g., IFS, Onestream, SAP, Oracle) and proficiency in Excel. - MustAttention to Detail & Accuracy – ensuring completeness and compliance in financial records.Problem-Solving & Investigative Skills – ability to identify, research, and resolve discrepancies.Time Management & Organization – managing multiple deadlines during month-end and audits.Communication Skills – effectively liaising with internal teams, auditors, banks, and regulatory bodies.Process Documentation – ability to create and maintain accounting process documentation.Team Collaboration – working closely with finance colleagues and cross-functional departments.Adaptability – flexibility to support ad-hoc requests and shifting priorities.The compensation for this position is anticipated to be between $ 70,000 and $ 75,000 annually, which includes base salary and target incentive. 📌 À titre de conseiller(-ère) en finances personnelles à la Caisse Desjardins de Sainte-Foy, vous êtes responsable de développer et de maintenir une relation d’affaires durable et empreinte de confiance avec les membres et clients dans le but d’assurer leur satisfaction. Votre rôle consiste à être à l’écoute des besoins des membres et clients, à leur offrir des conseils adaptés à leur situation financière et à les accompagner dans le choix de solutions appropriées leur permettant d’atteindre leurs objectifs financiers. Vous êtes responsable d’une clientèle composée de membres particuliers et entreprises et êtes le lien privilégié auprès de votre clientèle. La maîtrise des relations interpersonnelles constitue alors une compétence essentielle. Vous proposez et réalisez des offres liées au placement, au financement et à la protection, en mettant à profit votre aptitude à viser les résultats. Votre expertise vous permet d’assurer la cohérence entre la satisfaction des membres et clients, les objectifs d’affaires, la gestion saine et prudente des risques et la rentabilité. Vous entretenez des collaborations avec divers(e) partenaires et expert(e)s en vue de proposer des stratégies intégrées en réponse aux besoins des membres et clients. Plus spécifiquement, vous serez amené(e) à :Conseiller les membres et clients sur les meilleures stratégies et convenir des résultats visés.Agir à titre de responsable de la relation d’affaires, évaluer la satisfaction et la qualité du service, assurer les suivis et recommander les améliorations requises.Demeurer à l’affût de l’actualité financière, des tendances du marché et des meilleures pratiques ayant cours dans votre champ d’activité.Réaliser différentes activités de représentation et participer à la présence dans le milieu.Ce que nous offrons*Salaire concurrentiel et boni annuel4 semaines de vacances flexibles dès la première annéeRégime de retraite à prestations déterminées qui assure un revenu prévisible et stable durant toute la retraiteRégime d’assurance collective incluant des services de télémédecineRemboursement des frais liés à la santé, au bien-être et à de l’équipement pour le télétravailLes avantages sont applicables en fonction des critères d’admissibilité.Ce que vous mettrez à profit Baccalauréat dans une discipline appropriéeUn minimum d’une année d’expérience pertinenteVeuillez noter que d’autres combinaisons de formation et d’expérience pertinentes pourraient être considéréesCertificat de représentant(e) en épargne collective – Autorité des marchés financiersConnaissance du français nécessaireConnaissance de l'actualité financière, économique, politique et législativeConnaissance de l'ensemble des produits et services destinés aux membres de la Caisse et de ceux offerts par les centres Desjardins Entreprises, les À titre de conseiller(-ère) en finances personnelles à la Caisse Desjardins de Bellechasse, vous êtes responsable de développer et de maintenir une relation d’affaires durable et empreinte de confiance avec les membres et clients dans le but d’assurer leur satisfaction. Votre rôle consiste à être à l’écoute des besoins des membres et clients, à leur offrir des conseils adaptés à leur situation financière et à les accompagner dans le choix de solutions appropriées leur permettant d’atteindre leurs objectifs financiers. Vous êtes responsable d’une clientèle composée de membres particuliers et entreprises et êtes le lien privilégié auprès de votre clientèle. La maîtrise des relations interpersonnelles constitue alors une compétence essentielle. Vous proposez et réalisez des offres liées au placement, au financement et à la protection, en mettant à profit votre aptitude à viser les résultats. Votre expertise vous permet d’assurer la cohérence entre la satisfaction des membres et clients, les objectifs d’affaires, la gestion saine et prudente des risques et la rentabilité. Vous entretenez des collaborations avec divers partenaires et experts en vue de proposer des stratégies intégrées en réponse aux besoins des membres et clients. Plus spécifiquement, vous serez amené(e) à :Conseiller les membres et clients sur les meilleures stratégies et convenir des résultats visés.Agir à titre de responsable de la relation d’affaires, évaluer la satisfaction et la qualité du service, assurer les suivis et recommander les améliorations requises.Demeurer à l’affût de l’actualité financière, des tendances du marché et des meilleures pratiques ayant cours dans votre champ d’activité.Réaliser différentes activités de représentation et participer à la présence dans le milieu.Ce que nous offrons*Salaire concurrentiel et boni annuel4 About KinaxisElevate your career journey by embracing a new challenge with Kinaxis. We are experts in tech, but it’s really our people who give us passion to always seek ways to do things better. As such, we’re serious about your career growth and professional development, because People matter at Kinaxis. In 1984, we started out as a team of three engineers. Today, we have grown to become a global organization with over 2000 employees around the world, with a brand-new HQ based in Kanata North in Ottawa. As one of Canada’s Top Employers, we are proud to work with our customers and employees towards solving some of the biggest challenges facing supply chains today.At Kinaxis, we power the world’s supply chains to help preserve the planet’s resources and enrich the human experience. As a global leader in end-to-end supply chain management, we enable supply chain excellence for all industries, with more than 40,000 users in over 100 countries. We are expanding our team as we continue to innovate and revolutionize how we support our customers.LocationThis is a remote position. You can work from home and be located anywhere in Canada.If you are located in Ottawa, Canada you will be working a hybrid schedule of 4 days a week in office.About The TeamThe Senior Manager, Total Rewards leads the design, governance, and execution of global compensation and benefits programs that enable a high-performance, growth-oriented SaaS organization.This role is accountable for the operational management of the Total Rewards function, including global base pay structures, variable incentive plans (including sales compensation), equity programs, benefits strategy, and job architecture alignment. The Senior Manager translates business strategy, talent priorities, and market competitiveness into scalable, compliant, and data-driven reward solutions across jurisdictions.The role is accountable for functional outcomes, program governance, and the effective delivery of global Total Rewards operations.Vacancy StatusThis is an existing job vacancyWhat you will do Lead the design and governance of global compensation frameworks, ensuring market ‑ competitive and scalable pay practices across the organization Oversee global annual compensation planning cycles (merit, promotion, bonus, equity). Design and govern corporate incentive plans and global sales compensation programs. Oversee equity-based reward programs, ensuring governance and compliance Drive the institutionalization of job architecture (job families, roles, and levels) and embed it into systems, processes, and decision-making. Ensure compensation programs comply with applicable statutory, regulatory, and governance requirements, including pay‑equity analysis and reporting and pay transparency. Assist with executive and board compensation analysis, benchmarking, and governance processes in partnership with Finance and Legal. Provide leadership and direction on global benefits design and administration. Ensure benefit offerings are competitive, cost-effective, and compliant across multiple countries. Oversee vendor strategy, renewals, and performance management Evaluate program effectiveness through data and analytics and drive continuous improvement. Partner with Finance and SLT to help standardize various incentive plans and structures, measures, and governance processes including workforce cost planning and incentive modeling. Partner with Sales Leadership, Sales Operations and Finance to support the design and governance of sales incentive programs. Provide scenario modeling and analytics to inform business decisions Support global expansion and organizational scaling from a total rewards perspective. Establish and maintain Total Rewards policies, controls, and operational standards, ensuring regulatory compliance, audit readiness, and effective risk management across compensation, equity, and benefits programs. Partner with HR Technology to identify automation opportunities, reduce manual work, and enhance the manager and employee experience. Lead, develop, and performance manage a team of Total Rewards professionals, setting functional objectives, building capability, and ensuring high-quality execution across global programs. What we are looking for University degree in Human Resources, Business, Finance, Economics or related 7-10+ years of progressive experience in global Total Rewards including experience leading senior-level professionals and/or managers. Demonstrated expertise in global compensation benchmarking, sales compensation plan design, equity program governance and executive and board compensation, multi-country benefits strategy, compensation analytics and workforce cost modeling. (technology/SAAS sector preferred) Strong financial and business acumen including advanced understanding of Total Rewards strategy in recurring revenue business models Ability to operate effectively in complex, multi-jurisdictional environments. Strong analytical, problem-solving
